item.title: i know, i know, i'm late.
item.content:encoded: i am VERY late to the party, but may i ask if the idea was for the woman and 05-8 to represent the broken god and Yaldabaoth? cause i very much got that vibe from them. and may i ask if you have an idea of how the entity or whatever chooses the mutations that take place? like, why did monica become a living armory, why did norman become a eldrich psychic, why did ogre become ogre? is it personality, past or is it completely random?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great read! You guys did a fantastic job organizing and writing it all. Reminded me a bit of "Childhood's End" by Arthur C. Clarke. One thing I'm curious about though, is the empyrean parasite the "big brother" of O5-8? Or are we left to wonder what their relationship is…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A brilliant end to a brilliant series. I can see why you won the contest. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It makes us all really happy that our work could have that kind of effect on you, thanks for enjoying!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this whole project, it's been a week since I read this and I've routinely been asking myself if I would root for the anomaly in every article I read since.
This manages to kill the Foundation in a way that feels both realistic and fine, while extreme with the sheer number of casualties, but you guys made it feel like humanity still survived.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm at a loss for words at this point. This gave me the same feeling I get when I finish a giant 10-book fantasy series or something. Just kind of sat here not really sure what to do with myself now.

+1. That's all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you approve! It's always a risk using other people's characters, but Tilda Moose was the perfect fit to showcase the Thaumatology perspective from within the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This sure paid off.

(I'm tickled by the use of my avatar — really well done, too.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Swag! (also thanks for making your nicks the same so idiots like me won't get confused anymore :P )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That part was CC's and the dude is amazing at imagery. Glad you enjoyed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This ending gave me more chills than I can count, particularly this bit:

The man who was not the Foundation's Eighth Overseer armored himself in plates of the purest stone, hewn from the agony and valor of Earth's most sacred and worthy dead. He gathered what remained of the planet's oceans around him and rose. The last knight, the eternal guardian of the soul of humanity, ascended to fulfill his oath.

Upvoted with fervor

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fuckin' fantastic, a wonderful end to a wonderful series. The kids are all grown up :')

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An amazing end to a bunch of amazing tales. Great work team.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We definitely intend to go back and flesh out this setting we've created after the contest ends. Thanks for your good will! We're currently narrowly in second, so hopefully we can give winning our best shot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I do wish we could see what the "great threat" is (perhaps in a tale where the Apotheosis never took place, and the Foundation was Earth's only defense) the ending was clever, satisfying enough, and honestly downright beautiful. I'm truly glad we got to see the main cast return, and hope we might see some of them (Monica) return in your later tales. My only real problem is we never see what happened to poor Jake. Long story short, this series is by far the best Canon on this sight, and I hope y'all go far because of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So at the end of it all, the Foundation was truly in the wrong? Fascinating. This was marvelous and obtuse.

I love Armando, I'm glad to see he's doing well. :)

ghosthrowers

I also love this, but I also hate it? Like… Forgive me if you didn't come up with the term, but shouldn't there be an extra 't' in there? Because otherwise it's either "ghost" and "hrowers" or "ghos" and "throwers" and it's bothering me. :C Because it's just such a cool word.

Anyway, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking of Gainax, I actually was listening to Komm Süsser Tod on repeat while I wrote my parts of this. Nothing like listening to a jaunty showtune while the world comes crashing down.

We're glad you enjoyed :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems like they probably qualify at this point. I've used them twice in this series, Moose has used their AA a lot, and I'm sure someone else has used the character at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am the sorrow of all humanity. I am their pain, their endurance, their memory.

Something inside me tells me that this little line was all the authors' answer to what it was at the end.

E: I love this to no end. supervoted. So far, this entry gave the strongest end in my opinion. I love birdpocalypse and Lethe, but this went above and beyond. Pretty much Tengen Toppa Gurren Lagann levels of awesomeness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems like the logical conclusion to all of this. Done really stylishly too; I adore it.

How many more articles until Tilda Moose gets their own tag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a little disappointed earlier when I thought it would end with 3731, however this wonderfully tied together the entire story. Great job guys

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, whether we win or not, comment like this make this contest so rewarding. Just knowing we created something that people came to love is incredible. Thank you, and I’m happy you liked our story!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes.
The buildup to this was amazing.
It didn’t disappoint.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speechless. I could only really ever think that these types of complex and interlocking narratives could be told on the grand scale of a lengthy novel, but you all managed to pack all of it in six fantastic articles and tales.

This tale in particular blew me away in that the end of the world was loud, explosive, but heard from far away. The change of scope was executed perfectly. Couldn't have asked for a better finale.

item.title: Re: VICTORY
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much for your kind words!

item.title: VICTORY
item.content:encoded:
OH MY GOD.

GENIUS.

As I saw this series progress, I went from cold skepticism, to grudging admiration, and then to full on adoration. You have won the battle of the rating module, and my cold, grudging, heart. This is a perfect tapestry of what makes a beautiful tale, relatable characters, strangeness, and imagination beyond compare. Thank you for blessing this site with this beautiful artwork.

+1

;)

item.title: Co-Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
Here is the somber conclusion to the Apotheosis storyline, written by all four of us together. Weryl, Field, CC, y'all are the best and it was an absolute pleasure working together. Everyone else, please enjoy!

Image is from here and is cc by sa 2.0.
